FamilySyllabiThe McAdams Family - Weekly Lists
2025-2026
John Paul McAdamsSelect a one or two week list. If you select one week, only thefirst week of assignments below will be included on the list.Choose 'Yes' to have each day of the week on the printablelist separated by a page break.Select a start date for the lists below. Only a Monday - Fridaydate may be selected.
ReorderAssignmentsOn the Syllabi Progress list, you can drag a syllabus title tomove it above or below other syllabi titles on the list. Onceyou have adjusted the order of the syllabi to your liking, click"Reorder Assignments". This will reorder the assignmentsfound on the Assignment List (and your weekly list) to matchthe order displayed here.
Generate ListClick "Generate List" to open a new window containing aprintable list of the assignments below. You cannot click thebutton when you have unsaved settings on this page, so ifthe button is disabled, please make sure you have savedyour settings.

Joseph.raw_text:
Weekly List for Joseph McAdamsMonday, April 13, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Lesson 64Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 4Discuss the questions for Chapter 20, or write out your answers.1. Can you see, in verse 4, a sign of the catholicity of the Catholic Church?2. What miracle did Paul do at Troas?3. What are the important points of Paul’s discourse?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 10, Day: 3Today do workbook pages 86-88. (T.E. pages 162-167.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 3Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 3Lesson 20. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 2Do the first four of the Questions That Make You Think on pp. 60-61 in The Old World and America. Give the page numbers for thepages on which the answer is to be found. Remember, each answer should be three or more sentences long.Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 4Do the Review of Geology — Chapter 16 in your workbook.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 1 of 8
   Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 4Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.Music 7 (Alfred's Essentials of Music) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 5Listen to Bach's "Christmas Oratario"Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 2 of 8
     
 Tuesday, April 14, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 4Lesson 65English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 10, Day: 4Go over workbook page 89 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 90-91. (T.E. pages 168-173.)Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 2Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 9. If there are any you do not know, or can’t figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 4Lesson 20. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 3Pick a topic from the following suggestions, or come up with a topic of your own for a writing assignment (aim for 700 words):What was the Golden Age of Greece?Which are the most important battles in Greek history?Describe the art and architecture of Greece.The Gods of Greece.The Trojan War.A Profile of ...... (any of the important people you studied).Assessment Assignment:Weeks 14-16, Greece PaperReading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 3 of 8
        Wednesday, April 15, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Test 12Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 1Read Chapter 21 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Statues and Saints. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Do the Lesson 9 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 9 Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 5Lesson 20. Answer the questions in your workbook.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 1Work on the next 5 lines, up to “... deeds of mercy;” and review the first lines.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 4 of 8

Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. Did Paul know what awaited him at Jerusalem?2. What advice did those in Jerusalem give him?3. Did this appease those who were angry with Paul?4. What did those men intend to do to Paul? Why didn’t they do it?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 11, Day: 1Today start by doing workbook page 92. Then go over workbook page 93 with your teacher. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 23, Day: 1Attend Class. Do the Lesson XX Quia Games. Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 23, Day: 2Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.3. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 11, Day: 2Do workbook pages 95 and 96 today. (T.E. pages 180-183.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Take a spelling test on Lesson 9 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 23, Day: 2Lesson 21. Review first and second declension adjectives (lesson XX). Read the twenty-first lesson. Oral drill: decline the adjective“magnus, a, um” and the noun “servus, i.” Then put them together, remembering that “servus” is masculine. Do the Day 1 exercises.Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisservus, servi, servo, servum, servo, servi, servorum, servis, servos, servismagnus servus, magni servi, magno servo, magnum servum, magno servo, magni servi, magnorum servorum, magnis servis,magnos servos, magnis servisHistory & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 1Write.Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 23, Day: 3Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.4, 17.5, and 17.7. (Section 17.6 is optional). Answer the questions in your workbook.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 3Memorize and review for five minutes.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 7 of 8

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 64

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 20, or write out your answers.1. Can you see, in verse 4, a sign of the catholicity of the Catholic Church?2. What miracle did Paul do at Troas?3. What are the important points of Paul's discourse?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Today do workbook pages 86-88. (T.E. pages 162-167.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 20. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Do the first four of the Questions That Make You Think on pp. 60-61 in The Old World and America. Give the page numbers for thepages on which the answer is to be found. Remember, each answer should be three or more sentences long.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Review of Geology — Chapter 16 in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Listen to Bach's "Christmas Oratario"

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 65

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Go over workbook page 89 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 90-91. (T.E. pages 168-173.)

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 9. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 20. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Pick a topic from the following suggestions, or come up with a topic of your own for a writing assignment (aim for 700 words):What was the Golden Age of Greece?Which are the most important battles in Greek history?Describe the art and architecture of Greece.The Gods of Greece.The Trojan War.A Profile of ...... (any of the important people you studied).Assessment Assignment:Weeks 14-16, Greece Paper

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Test 12

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 21 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Statues and Saints. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Lesson 9 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 9 Key Words you had trouble spelling.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 20. Take Quiz 20.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Write.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.1 and 17.2. Answer the questions in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work on the next 5 lines, up to "... deeds of mercy;" and review the first lines.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 66

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 21. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. Did Paul know what awaited him at Jerusalem?2. What advice did those in Jerusalem give him?3. Did this appease those who were angry with Paul?4. What did those men intend to do to Paul? Why didn't they do it?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Today start by doing workbook page 92. Then go over workbook page 93 with your teacher. Finally, do workbook page 94. (T.E. pages174-179.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 9 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Attend Class. Do the Lesson XX Quia Games.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.3. Answer the questions in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 16, Bartoleme Esteban Murillo.

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Finish listening to the "Christmas Oratorio" by Bach.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 67

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 22 of Acts. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do workbook pages 95 and 96 today. (T.E. pages 180-183.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on Lesson 9 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 21. Review first and second declension adjectives (lesson XX). Read the twenty-first lesson. Oral drill: decline the adjective"magnus, a, um" and the noun "servus, i." Then put them together, remembering that "servus" is masculine. Do the Day 1 exercises.Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisservus, servi, servo, servum, servo, servi, servorum, servis, servos, servismagnus servus, magni servi, magno servo, magnum servum, magno servo, magni servi, magnorum servorum, magnis servis,magnos servos, magnis servis

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Write.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.4, 17.5, and 17.7. (Section 17.6 is optional). Answer the questions in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Optional: Do Art Pad #16, Genre Scene.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdams
Monday, April 13, 2026
Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 64
Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Discuss the questions for Chapter 20, or write out your answers.1. Can you see, in verse 4, a sign of the catholicity of the Catholic Church?2. What miracle did Paul do at Troas?3. What are the important points of Paul's discourse?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Today do workbook pages 86-88. (T.E. pages 162-167.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 20. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.
History & Geog 7 (Old World & America)Do the first four of the Questions That Make You Think on pp. 60-61 in The Old World and America. Give the page numbers for thepages on which the answer is to be found. Remember, each answer should be three or more sentences long.

(any of the important people you studied).Assessment Assignment:Weeks 14-16, Greece Paper

Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Read Chapter 21 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Statues and Saints. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.

Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 21. Review first and second declension adjectives (lesson XX). Read the twenty-first lesson. Oral drill: decline the adjective"magnus, a, um" and the noun "servus, i." Then put them together, remembering that "servus" is masculine. Do the Day 1 exercises.Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisservus, servi, servo, servum, servo, servi, servorum, servis, servos, servismagnus servus, magni servi, magno servo, magnum servum, magno servo, magni servi, magnorum servorum, magnis servis,magnos servos, magnis servis
